headline: National Digital Infrastructure Audit Reveals 450 Vulnerabilities
context: Comprehensive audit of Malaysia digital infrastructure identified 450 critical vulnerabilities across government and essential services.

cards.big: National audit found 450 critical vulnerabilities in digital infrastructure
cards.sub: Assessment covered 800 government systems and 120 essential service networks

cards.big: 85 vulnerabilities classified as immediately exploitable with high impact
cards.sub: 28 systems had known vulnerabilities unpatched for more than 2 years

cards.big: Healthcare and education systems accounted for 40% of critical findings
cards.sub: Both sectors had lowest cybersecurity staffing and budget allocations

cards.big: NACSA given 90-day deadline to remediate immediately exploitable vulnerabilities
cards.sub: Full remediation programme estimated at RM2.8 billion over 3 years

cards.big: Infrastructure audit tests whether vulnerability disclosure leads to actual remediation or just reporting
450 digital infrastructure vulnerabilities found. Audit findings matter only if remediation follows within fixed deadlines.
